techniques.name: Application Layer Protocol
techniques.description: Adversaries may communicate using application layer protocols to avoid detection.

techniques.name: Proxy
techniques.description: Adversaries may route network traffic through proxy infrastructure to conceal command and control communications.

techniques.name: Encrypted Channel
techniques.description: Adversaries may use encrypted channels to hide command and control activity from network monitoring.

techniques.name: Phishing
techniques.description: Adversaries may send phishing messages with malicious links or attachments to gain access.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
techniques.description: Adversaries may abuse command and scripting interpreters to execute malicious code.

techniques.name: Scheduled Task/Job
techniques.description: Adversaries may schedule tasks or jobs for delayed execution and persistence.

techniques.name: Obfuscated/Compressed Files and Information
techniques.description: Adversaries may obfuscate or compress payloads to hinder analysis and detection.

techniques.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
techniques.description: Adversaries may transfer tools or payloads from external systems into a compromised environment.

techniques.name: System Services
techniques.description: Adversaries may use system services to execute malicious programs.

techniques.name: Valid Accounts
techniques.description: Adversaries may use compromised legitimate credentials to blend in with normal activity.

techniques.name: Remote Services
techniques.description: Adversaries may use remote services to move laterally between systems.

techniques.name: Masquerading
techniques.description: Adversaries may disguise artifacts as legitimate files or processes.

techniques.name: User Execution
techniques.description: Adversaries may rely on user interaction to execute malicious content.

techniques.name: Process Injection
techniques.description: Adversaries may inject code into running processes to evade defenses and execute payloads.

techniques.name: System Information Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may collect host and operating system details for planning follow-on actions.

techniques.name: File and Directory Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may enumerate files and directories to identify targets and staging locations.

techniques.name: Process Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may enumerate running processes to identify security tools and opportunities.

techniques.name: Remote System Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may discover remote systems on the network for lateral movement.

techniques.name: System Network Configuration Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may gather network configuration details from compromised hosts.

techniques.name: System Network Connections Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may inspect active network connections and listening ports.

techniques.name: Indicator Removal on Host
techniques.description: Adversaries may remove forensic artifacts to reduce evidence of compromise.

techniques.name: Modify Registry
techniques.description: Adversaries may modify the registry to alter system behavior or persistence.

techniques.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution
techniques.description: Adversaries may configure programs to run automatically at boot or user logon.

techniques.name: Create or Modify System Process
techniques.description: Adversaries may create or modify system-level processes and services for persistence.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
techniques.description: Adversaries may exfiltrate data using uncommon or non-primary protocols.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
techniques.description: Adversaries may exfiltrate data over established command and control channels.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
techniques.description: Adversaries may exfiltrate data to web services or cloud-hosted platforms.

techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
techniques.description: Adversaries may exploit internet-facing applications to gain initial access.

techniques.name: External Remote Services
techniques.description: Adversaries may use exposed remote access services to enter victim environments.

techniques.name: Brute Force
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt many passwords or credentials to obtain account access.

techniques.name: OS Credential Dumping
techniques.description: Adversaries may dump operating system credential material for privilege escalation.

techniques.name: Account Manipulation
techniques.description: Adversaries may manipulate account settings or permissions to maintain access.

techniques.name: Impair Defenses
techniques.description: Adversaries may disable or tamper with security controls to avoid detection.

techniques.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
techniques.description: Adversaries may encrypt victim data to disrupt operations and demand ransom.

techniques.name: Non-Standard Port
techniques.description: Adversaries may use uncommon ports for command and control to evade network defenses.

techniques.name: Acquire Infrastructure
techniques.description: Adversaries may obtain infrastructure such as servers, domains, or botnets for operations.

techniques.name: Establish Accounts
techniques.description: Adversaries may create and maintain accounts to support phishing, infrastructure operations, or persistence.

techniques.name: Web Service
techniques.description: Adversaries may use web services as dead drops or command and control channels.
